about.source: BfArM 'Qualitätssicherung der eingelieferten Genomdaten durch Genomrechenzentren (GRZ)', MV GenomSeq, Version 01.4 (01.06.2026), Abschnitt A / Tabelle 1.
about.applies_to: GRZ Datenkranz (top-level submission + donors[]). Off unless enabled with --grz-rules.
about.scope_note: Only the JSON-checkable Grundprüfung criteria (Tabelle 1) are encoded. The other Grundprüfung items (file sizes, checksum agreement, read counts R1=R2 / lines divisible by 4) and the Detailprüfung QC thresholds (Tabelle 2/3: mean depth, read length, %bases>=Q30/Q20, target-region coverage) operate on the raw FASTQ/BAM data or on reported lab-QC metrics against a context matrix (diseaseType x libraryType x tumor/normal/germline) and are out of scope for a pure-JSON validator — run the GRZ QC pipeline (github.com/BfArM-MVH/GRZ_QC_Workflow) for those.
about.engine: primitives are implemented in rules.py; the engine reads id/primitive/severity/params only.

rules.doc: Tabelle 1 #1: for rare disease, panel and WES are excluded -> donors[].labData[].libraryType in {wgs, wgs_lr}. (Note: GRZ excludes 'none', unlike KDK criterion 10.)
Tabelle 1 #2: submission.genomicDataCenterId must equal the receiving GRZ's own id. Supply via --rules-config {genomic_data_center_id:'...'}.
Tabelle 1 #3: donors[].researchConsents[].noScopeJustification limited to patient-related reasons; the two technical/organizational values are errors.

allowed: patient did not return consent documents
